title: podman rm fail when using container name
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

/kind bug


**Description**
Podman not able to identify that the container with specific name exists, hence removing container using name failed, while removing using container ID worked fine
<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->
```
❯ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                    COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS   PORTS                                           NAMES
95a3cd3e7ecf  docker.io/jaegertracing/all-in-one:1.18  --sampling.strate...  23 minutes ago  Created  0.0.0.0:5775->5775/udp, 0.0.0.0:5778->5778/tcp  jaeger
❯ sudo podman rm jaeger
Error: no container with name or ID jaeger found: no such container
❯ podman rm 95a3cd3e7ecf
95a3cd3e7ecf877be5e6c6aa143f1576726d32e033ac60ca72e1187ed0ee6f45

```

comments.body: Are you running this as a rootless user?
Do you have a way to repeat this?

Yes, please see issue  [containers/podman] podman creating improperly
configured containers (#7260)

Environment : f32, running rootless, no other operation other than ones
mentioned in #7260


On Sat, 8 Aug, 2020, 4:34 PM Daniel J Walsh, <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> Do you have a way to repeat this?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you authored the thread.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7259#issuecomment-670890169>,
> or unsubscribe
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AFJPVWBTYNKPAJ77ZOX6PSDR7UWJ3ANCNFSM4PYBPDPQ>
> .
>

@ideepika I'm not sure this is an issue. I tried reproducing it, see the results below.

```
[edeediong@kontrol ~]$ podman run -d --name redis redis
a677e179de1b4bf5f9ef9bd7d951ffc537640bd45875d3d8588ea6d86ae3501b
[edeediong@kontrol ~]$ podman stop redis
a677e179de1b4bf5f9ef9bd7d951ffc537640bd45875d3d8588ea6d86ae3501b
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND       CREATED             STATUS                     PORTS   NAMES
a677e179de1b  docker.io/library/redis:latest  redis-server  About a minute ago  Exited (0) 21 seconds ago          redis
[edeediong@kontrol ~]$ podman rm redis
a677e179de1b4bf5f9ef9bd7d951ffc537640bd45875d3d8588ea6d86ae3501b
[edeediong@kontrol ~]$ podman ps -a
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE   COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS   NAMES
[edeediong@kontrol ~]$ 
```

So can you please check your setup again?
Seems like #7260 is closed and we have a way to remove improper containers, I am working on making this easier in the future.
